$comment: WorldBuild Bench model registry. pricing is USD per million tokens (editable — Anthropic rows verified against the official price sheet 2026-07-03; non-Anthropic rows verified against provider/OpenRouter listings 2026-07-03 — OpenRouter prices float by routed provider, and MiniMax M3 ($0.30/$1.20 launch discount, $0.60/$2.40 list) and Qwen3.7 Max ($1.25/$3.75 promo, $2.50/$7.50 list) carry time-limited discounts; Claude Sonnet 5 is intro-priced $2/$10 through 2026-08-31 then $3/$15; Gemini 3.1 Pro bills 2x input / 1.5x output above 200K-token prompts, which this flat pricing does not model — re-verify every row before publishing a round). base_url_env, when set and present in the environment, overrides base_url. provider is one of: anthropic | openai | google. The openai provider serves any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (OpenRouter, Groq, Cerebras) via base_url; api.openai.com itself goes through the Responses API (current OpenAI reasoning models reject function tools + reasoning effort on chat/completions). thinking: 'adaptive' enables Anthropic adaptive thinking on models that support it (reasoning-by-default parity with the other providers). max_output_tokens is the per-turn output cap sent to the provider: min(the model's documented max completion tokens, a 32768 practical ceiling — the adapters are non-streaming with a 600s per-attempt fetch window, so longer single replies would abort on timeout; Anthropic rows are 16384 because the direct Anthropic API requires streaming for requests that may exceed 10 minutes). Documented max completion tokens verified against the OpenRouter models API 2026-07-04: GLM-5.2 128000, MiniMax M3 512000, Qwen3.7 Max 65536, Kimi K2.7 Code 16384 (the one binding value), DeepSeek V4 Pro 384000, GPT-5.5 128000, Gemini 3.1 Pro 65536, Claude Sonnet 5 / Opus 4.8 / Fable 5 128000, Haiku 4.5 64000. Reasoning tokens count against the cap where billed as output. Cache pricing (input_cache_read_per_mtok / input_cache_write_per_mtok) verified against the OpenRouter models API 2026-07-04: Anthropic reads are 0.1x input and 5-minute cache writes 1.25x input per the official price sheet; GPT-5.5 cached input is 10% of list; Gemini 3.1 Pro implicit-caching reads are $0.20/M (explicit-cache storage fees not modeled). Cached rates matter for the direct-API rows — where the provider returns exact cost (OpenRouter) it overrides the table. reasoning_effort (low|medium|high|xhigh), when set on a row, requests that effort tier via the provider's native control (OpenAI Responses reasoning.effort, OpenRouter reasoning.effort, Anthropic output_config.effort, Gemini thinkingLevel — xhigh clamps to HIGH); unset = provider default. wb run --effort overrides per run; the tier used is recorded in result.json. vision: true marks models that accept image input (all Anthropic rows, GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.1 Pro per provider docs; OpenRouter rows verified against the OpenRouter models API input_modalities 2026-07-05: minimax-m3 and kimi-k2.7-code accept images; glm-5.2, qwen3.7-max, deepseek-v4-pro are text-only). Vision models receive the test_game playtest screenshots (2s / post-input / 10s JPEGs) attached after the JSON report; text-only models get the identical JSON report with no images — whether a run had visual feedback is recorded in result.json (model.vision, agent.images_sent).
